reviews.review_description: Playing Call of Duty zombies in VR is incredible, this review is more for the modders than the actual game. The gun mechanics are fantastic, playing this game genuinely made me a better shot in real life and helped me practice shooting eith both eyes open. I haven't really touched the multiplayer but it's worth the price of admission for the mods.

I used to play this game all day soon as i came home from school  i would play this game the mods made the game much better but now ever since they added contractors showdown the game has been crashing plus you cant even load the game no more because you will crash instantly DON'T WASTE YOUR MONEY

This game has the best modding community in vr, just behind Pavlov, b&s, and bonelab. The mods are amazing. An exact 1:1 replica of CodZombies, an amazing TF2 mod, best star wars battlefront vr game, amazing war hammer mod. Every big mod in this game is amazing. This is my favorite game on vr. I have about 300 vr games, and i played vr for years. But the sad part about this game is that it wasn't the same amount of players. Contractors player base died. The only thing keeping the amazing player base up is the modding community. The Devs of contractors stopped updating the game for so long. I highly recommend this game for the mods. But the players are mostly gone.

To preface my criticisms i will say that I have had this game for close to a year now, (i run it on my quest 3)  when everything runs as intended, its incredibly fun. While the base game isn't the best, the mods are nothing short of amazing. One mod to the next make me feel as though I'm playing an Entirely different game, which i love.  I truly don't understand how some people can call it boring. However, the devs have Completely abandoned this game, they apparently have no intention of fixing the bugs which Become more numerous by the day. It takes multiple attempts to join a modded lobby, and even when i manage to join a game i never fail to crash mid way through the round. By the time i got this game, the golden age had already passed, but even so, i fell in love with it. I cant express how disappointed i am in the devs. The game is getting to the point were its unplayable, my cash only bought me a few months worth of smooth gamplay until the issues piled up. i want a refund. I urge you not to buy this game.

Most new players jump in without ever realizing what this game actually is.

They load into a map with the wrong setup, no proper loadout, no weapons, no understanding of how the system works—then they wonder why they’re getting destroyed before they even have a chance to figure it out.

What they don’t understand is this isn’t just another fast-paced shooter where you trade lives back and forth until the match ends. This world keeps expanding. New maps, bigger maps, different styles of combat, custom loadouts, experiences inspired by games like Fortnite and Call of Duty, even entire environments that feel like they belong in another universe.

I’ve been playing since release, and I’m still finding things I didn’t know existed.

The first time I tried the Star Wars loadout, I was honestly caught off guard by how intense it was. It felt like stepping into something completely different—like the game had changed again overnight.

And the weapons… there are more than most people realize. Makarovs, CZs, Kimbers, Heckler & Kochs, .50 BMGs, M60s, M249 SAWs with 200-round drums, WWII weapons, knives you can actually throw to survive, vehicles that completely change how you play—it feels like if it exists somewhere in history, it probably exists here too.

The problem is most people never stay long enough to discover it.

They quit before they learn how to unlock the right gear. Before they understand the maps. Before they find the loadout that makes the game click.

But once it clicks… it’s over.

You’ll start planning your day around it. You’ll tell yourself “just one more round.” Hours disappear. Your job starts asking questions. Your wife starts packing boxes. And suddenly it’s just you… and this masterpiece that somehow keeps getting bigger, stranger, and better every time you log in.

Find your niche, and I promise—you’ll understand.

And when you do, you’ll become one of us.

I’m Stiffler.

And if you see Spider, BillGates, Predator, Belle, Sancho, J Geer, Newbee, or that little skeleton guy under this review…

watch your back.

The dying community and mods breaking is the kicker, and the bug fixes are long over due. 

Love the single player modes tho and some of the mods are mind blowing. Ive played star wars, cod zombies, and halo VR natively on quest using just mods which is absolutely incredible. I'm sure this would be a fantastic game if cared for a little more.

I bought this game for its mods and my favorite and the most popular one is the Star Wars Battlefront mod. The game modder somehow managed to accurately bring Star Wars Battlefront to VR. Plus there are others like TF2, and COD. I love what the devs have done with the community letting make their own mods.

not so long ago this game was so amazing. lots of great mods. thriving community. pretty smooth game play. and then the developers focused their attention on their showdown version of this game, which is fine because that was a great game too. but then they stop developing this game. game mods started breaking. the smooth performance was no longer, now it's very choppy and unplayable. the game crashes constantly. and it's not just me, i have a pretty sizable group of friends who play this game and they say the same thing. and the community left. you're lucky to see a server that has more than a few people playing. and everyone is complaining how bad and choppy this game is. such a shame because this game was awesome once upon a time. do not waste your money on this vaporware.

This game is literally meant for any cod fan, cod zombies fan, Star Wars fan, guns and shooting fan, ww1 and ww2 nerds, team fortress fans, and just fast paced death match style games. Your basically just playing a og cod game in vr, screaming lobbies after matches, cursing ( you can turn voice chat off) there are great mods with NO pc or any thing required, you just download the game and you get to download mods from in the game, no sign up nothing. It has been forgotten by the devs for almost two years, but it’s still really fun. You can also be a storm trooper

reviews.review_title: Good but In a dead state
reviews.review_description:
Contractors is a great game, honestly, but the main issue with this game is that it is abandoned. There are zero new updates and zero new content, so you get what you have and that's it.

Mods:
This is the main reason why I play the game and why most people still play this game. There are a lot of maps, weapons, sounds, and even game types. Personally, I play CODZ, COD4, and MW2 most of the time. MW2 even has a level progression system, the same for weapons, and most of the weapons work fine and can be used well. So, mods are a big reason to get this game and the only reason why I would recommend it.

Multiplayer:
I rarely see someone playing the regular Contractors Multiplayer or maps. As I mentioned previously, it's mostly mods. They have the normal types of game modes: TDM, S&D, KC, etc.
Depending on the hour you are playing, you will either find empty lobbies or some full lobbies full of "sweaty" players, so you're going to have a hard time enjoying those matches if you are not used to VR FPS.
Still, you can play alone with bots or friends if you like that; no shame in that.

Bugs/Issues:
So far, in the time that I have been playing, there are some big issues/bugs in the current state of the game. Sometimes you won't be able to shoot or rotate while moving or at all, so this is one of the worst issues currently because it can make you lose while playing zombies or in PvP.
Sometimes you appear with no gear and you don't have any other option than to just get killed and hope you respawn with your weapons.
The game servers sometimes won't work to host a game. Sometimes I keep pressing start and getting a black screen as if it's about to load, or sometimes I'm already in the match and it will just kick me back to the lobby. Sometimes changing the server region helps, but still, this is an issue.

Community:
It's VR, and you're going to get the expected type of players: kids, toxic teenagers or toxic adults, and overall disgraceful people screaming insults. Personally, just kick them if you are the host or mute them. But I have also gotten chill lobbies where people are just enjoying the game and having fun.

I feel like if they put more love into this game, people would come back to it, but if you are thinking of getting it, I've already told you what to expect. I personally play some days, set up an MW2 mod lobby with bots; sometimes people join and other times they don't, but I still have fun, or I play COD zombies with my friends. The mod community is the best because of the content they bring, and if you are a modder, thank you!

So I’ve been playing the game for over a year now, and I have to say it’s very fun and very entertaining. However I ran into a big issue, ￼￼ even though I have already downloaded the mods, including CODZ zombies, I can’t play them offline. And that really sucks to like an insane extent. If you could do your best to try and make it so we can play the mods offline, that would be very appreciated and I would be very very very happy 🙂 ￼

Contractors VR is the most Call of Duty-like shooter you'll find on Quest, which isn't saying as much as it should. The gunplay is excellent, solid and satisfying with headshots that matter. There's a healthy player base thanks to cross-play, proper loadout systems, and enough game modes to keep things interesting. 

It's got a welcome dose of irreverence too, letting you mess about in the hub rather than taking itself too seriously like Onward does. But the jank is everywhere. Character models with inverse kinematics look like epileptic Dolly Parton cosplaying as Master Chief. Other players warp and contort at impossible angles, turning tense firefights into unintentional comedy. 

Positional audio is rubbish to non-existent, making it hard to locate where fights are happening. The menus and UI look placeholder-ish, and there's no excuse for that. Skip the bot mode entirely, it's still in the cupboard. With real players it can be a huge amount of fun, but those technical shortcomings hold it back from being what it should be. 

For the full review, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews Contractors VR.

reviews.review_title: The Only Quest2,3.3s fps you need 100%
reviews.review_description:
If you love fun call of duty type and style of games especially any modern  type fps this has it all. I can't believe I waited so long to buy it. Best £15 I've ever spent in vr. Its got single player, coop and  full mp round up just like cod games survival is just awesome and again they have obviously took inspiration from cod survival with there own vision for vr. Its well made well balanced the gun handling and mechanics are very good. Every update so far has brought life to the game and new improvements. Hats off to the dev team this really is a great fun game to spend hours and hours playing
TO THE DEVS
1/. I would like to see is some sort of new ranked system with progression with leaderboards etc. Its a big ask but gives people reason to keep playing and improving team comunications and overall ability. Will also help with the esports arena.
2/. Weapon skins.
3/. 3d Face scan your own face onto your character. Like on rainbow 6 vegas2 on xbox when it came out.
ombie waves on all base maps with coop type perks and weapon progression.
5/.killstreak offensive// perk system with tablet R.C drivable c4 buggy, weaponised 
R.C. air drone that auto follows and protects you or take control on tablet and go shooting from the air.
Deployable auto turret
Defensive//  deployable health regeneration granade.
6/. Deployable body armour for your team mates.
Defensive handheld Shields to block a lot of projectiles. helmets on players to reduce headset damage and my final wish is for 
7/.visual bullet holes on surfaces instead of little puffs of contact smoke especially when in close quarters and medium range. I've asked this last as its eagerly wanted and I think the easiest to impliment.
Only issue I've had with the game is the thumbs tips randomly twitch about which ruins the realism sometimes and I am sure will be an easy fix. So other than that minor gripe not much to complain about.
This latest update with the night sky and target practice birds are a real treat btw.
Also when you shoot basket balls they should either move or explode please.

This game is a 9.5 out of 10 for me. It will be a 10/10 if you implement even one of my crazy suggestions.

Implementing mods for the quest2 has been hugely enjoyable. And as a lifelong call of duty player wanting to play cod in vr has now been realised. Salute to the cod map modder. Good job.

Since I wrote this the game has still surprised me. We now have our first weapon skins introduced by the Halloween 🎃 zomkin update and event to unlock them. Great idea devs and I really enjoyed the 2nd zombies map. Never had so much fun with 3 guys with guns. Knife skin desert Eagle and sniper weapon skins unlocked by popping 🎃 off zombie heads.  Great job guys 10/10
And thankyou for all the bits I asked for especially weapon skins and another zombie base map. Keep up the great work.

1 last ask to the devs 1 heavy machine gun and
And 1 shotgun with a drum magazine please for coop and zombies.

Also 2024 Halloween season are we getting zomkin nights again and can we please have another coop zombies map. I beg you.
I've played on 1quest 2,3 and 3s as well as pcvr and on all systems it performs exceptional. As long as the devs do little events now and then to keep the game current I think it will be a strong game for another 5 years at least.

reviews.review_description:
Update for 2025-26
This was a great game. Devs gave us a last update that was a bug itself and abandoned us. Now the game continously crashes and you stick to walls if you get too close. The servers are hard to get started... we try moving server locations and all these wives'tales fixes but its all the same. Its become a disappointment and my confidence in this company has been diminished for leaving it this way before abandoning it. 

2023 Review here: 
The biggest bang for your buck. Works well, bugs are always being worked on. Its not perfect, but can be hours of fun plus tons of free moded content. Team Deathmatch and Domination just like the Call of Duty days... with much more of course.

reviews.review_title: Good game, but some work is needed.
reviews.review_description: So, I hope this review is not misunderstood since the game could be great for many players who do not get motion sickness, and also for those who just use a controller and not a physical gunstock. The game graphics are very good; however, I cannot talk much about them since I don't get to play for long because the game does not address the issue of motion sickness by maybe implementing a vignette. A vignette narrows your field of view when the player is in motion (walking or running), so it helps reduce the feeling of getting disoriented. Could graphics be improved? I am sure they could, but compared with some other games like "Tactical Assault" and others, the graphics are very good. The one other thing that is not a major issue, since not everyone uses a gunstock, is that they are missing horizontal rotational axes of adjustment for those who use a physical gunstock to create a more immersive experience. I use one and noticed that the in-game AR rifle, when I am pointing straight while standing squarely, with the physical gunstock, it is slightly pointing to the left by some degrees. You can still play the game very well, but it is not a one-to-one alignment. Outside of those things, I think the game it worth the investment if you like FPS games that have a realistic look and that you can have many gun setups. Also it is great that you can have your own private server to play with friends or just again the bot.
